meta.description: State archetype nodes for the sliding window family. Each archetype defines a canonical computational state shape, fill order, feasibility envelope, and required invariants.

field_notes.requires: Invariant IDs that must hold for this archetype to produce correct results.
field_notes.uses: Transition archetype IDs that drive this state's computation.
field_notes.commonly_confused_with: Other archetype IDs that solvers frequently conflate with this one.
field_notes.verified_by: Verifier check IDs executed during Stage 5.
field_notes.confidence_weight: Points awarded to the confidence score when this archetype is confirmed and verified.

nodes.label: Fixed-size sliding window
nodes.state_form: window [r-k+1, r] moves right; maintain aggregate over k elements
nodes.fill_order: r = k-1 to n-1, one step right per iteration; l = r-k+1 is derived
nodes.feasibility: O(n) time, O(1) auxiliary space (O(k) if storing window elements)
nodes.canonical_problem: Maximum sum subarray of size k

nodes.label: Variable-size sliding window (two-pointer)
nodes.state_form: window [l, r]; expand r greedily, shrink l when constraint violated
nodes.fill_order: r = 0 to n-1 outer loop; l advances independently on violation; total moves O(n)
nodes.feasibility: O(n) amortized — l and r each traverse the array at most once
nodes.canonical_problem: Longest subarray with sum at most k

nodes.label: Sliding window with monotone deque
nodes.state_form: deque D holds indices in decreasing value order; D.front() is argmax of current window [l, r]
nodes.fill_order: r = 0 to n-1; pop back while arr[D.back()] <= arr[r]; push r; pop front while D.front() < l
nodes.feasibility: O(n) total — each index pushed and popped at most once
nodes.space_optimization: Deque stores indices only, not values

nodes.label: Count subarrays satisfying constraint — at-most-k subtraction trick
nodes.state_form: count(at_most_k) - count(at_most_(k-1)); each count uses sa_sliding_window_variable
nodes.canonical_problem: Number of subarrays with at most k distinct characters
